title: Falsos Cognatos
description: 25 falsos cognatos clássicos que enganam brasileiros — a palavra parece uma coisa, mas significa outra.

cards.example: I thought he was French, but actually he's Belgian.
The kids pretended to be pirates.
Push the door to open it.
My parents live in São Paulo.
I borrowed three books from the library.
This dress is made of a soft fabric.
The hotel served an exquisite dinner.
The professor gave a lecture on climate change.
She's studying biology in college.
He wore a superhero costume to the party.

cards.back: finalmente, no fim (NÃO é 'eventualmente' — eventualmente = occasionally)
cards.example: Keep trying and you'll eventually succeed.

cards.back: comparecer, frequentar (NÃO é 'atender' — atender telefone = answer)
cards.example: I couldn't attend the meeting yesterday.

cards.back: ajudar, auxiliar (NÃO é 'assistir' — assistir TV = watch)
cards.example: A nurse assisted the doctor during the surgery.
The meeting will resume after lunch.

cards.back: perceber, notar; aviso (NÃO é 'notícia' — notícia = news)
cards.example: I didn't notice you had changed your hair.

cards.back: massa, macarrão (NÃO é 'pasta' — pasta de arquivos = folder)
cards.example: We had pasta with tomato sauce for dinner.

cards.back: romance (livro) (NÃO é 'novela' — novela de TV = soap opera)
cards.example: She's writing her first novel.
Staying home in the storm was a sensible decision.

cards.back: apoiar, sustentar (NÃO é 'suportar/aguentar' — suportar = stand, bear)
cards.example: My family supports my decision to study abroad.

cards.back: perceber, dar-se conta (NÃO é 'realizar' — realizar sonho = fulfill, achieve)
cards.example: I realized I had forgotten my keys.
We must fight prejudice in all its forms.
He suffered a knee injury during the game.

cards.back: aperto, pegada; segurar firme (NÃO é 'gripe' — gripe = flu)
cards.example: She kept a firm grip on the handrail.

cards.back: refeitório, cantina (NÃO é 'cafeteria' — café/cafeteria = coffee shop)
cards.example: The students had lunch in the school cafeteria.

cards.back: prever, esperar (NÃO é 'antecipar/adiantar' — antecipar = bring forward)
cards.example: We anticipate heavy traffic this weekend.
